abstract: Group of 7 major industrial nations meets in Washington under extraordinary security; four-year-old computer records suggest Al Qaeda might single out World Bank and International Monetary Fund for terrorist action; China is invited to dine with member nations, event seen as first step toward admission and belated acknowledgment of its huge role in world economy; World Bank leader James D Wolfensohn presides over what could be his last annual meeting; his term as president ends in May and he is not expected to be asked to stay on; IMF's new managing director Rodrigo de Rato makes impressive debut with his straight talk (M)
